source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 14.3.2 Query package (pp. 258-259)

questions.why: -q queries and -a means all installed packages.

questions.q: Show detailed information about the installed package bash.
questions.why: -qi queries the info of an installed package.

questions.q: List the files installed by the package bash.
questions.why: -ql lists the files a package released onto the system.

questions.q: Find out which package owns the file /usr/bin/bash.
questions.why: -qf queries the package that a given installed file belongs to.

options: The dependencies of a package (add -p to query an uninstalled .rpm file's dependencies)
questions.why: -qR lists dependencies; combined with -p (e.g. rpm -qRp file.rpm) it queries a not-yet-installed package file.

questions.q: What does adding -p to a query (e.g. rpm -qip file.rpm) do?
options: Queries an UNINSTALLED package file rather than the installed database
Prints the package to the screen
questions.why: -p targets a package file on disk (uninstalled), so rpm -qip/-qlp inspect a .rpm before installing it.

questions.q: List all installed packages whose name starts with 'dbus' (wildcards aren't allowed in rpm, so filter with grep).
questions.why: rpm queries need a deterministic name, so pipe rpm -qa through grep to match a pattern.
